questions.text: Which of the following is the oldest landmass of India?
questions.explanation: The Peninsular Plateau is one of the ancient landmasses on the earth's surface and was part of the Gondwana land.

questions.text: The northernmost range of the Himalayas is known as:
questions.explanation: The northernmost range is known as the Inner Himalayas or the Himadri. It is the most continuous range.

questions.text: What is the average height of the peaks in the Himadri range?
questions.explanation: The Himadri contains all prominent Himalayan peaks with an average height of 6,000 metres.

questions.text: The range lying to the south of the Himadri is known as:
questions.explanation: The range lying to the south of the Himadri forms the most rugged mountain system and is known as Himachal or lesser Himalaya.

questions.text: Which of the following is the longest and the most important range of the Himachal?
questions.explanation: The Pir Panjal range forms the longest and the most important range in the Lesser Himalayas.

questions.text: The outermost range of the Himalayas is called:
questions.explanation: The outermost range of the Himalayas is called the Shiwaliks.

questions.text: Longitudinal valleys lying between lesser Himalaya and the Shiwaliks are known as:
questions.explanation: Duns like Dehra Dun, Kotli Dun and Patli Dun are longitudinal valleys between Lesser Himalayas and Shiwaliks.

questions.text: The part of the Himalayas lying between Indus and Satluj is known as:
questions.explanation: Traditionally, the part between Indus and Satluj is called Punjab Himalayas, but also locally known as Kashmir and Himachal Himalaya.

questions.text: The Himalayas lying between Satluj and Kali rivers is known as:
questions.explanation: The part of the Himalayas lying between Satluj and Kali rivers is known as Kumaon Himalayas.

questions.text: The Brahmaputra marks the ________ boundary of the Himalayas.
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra river marks the easternmost boundary of the Himalayas.

questions.text: The Northern Plain is formed by the interplay of which three major river systems?
questions.explanation: The plain is formed by the Indus, the Ganga and the Brahmaputra along with their tributaries.

questions.text: What type of soil is the Northern Plain made of?
questions.explanation: The Northern Plain is formed of alluvial soil deposited over millions of years.

questions.text: A narrow belt of about 8 to 16 km in width lying parallel to the slopes of the Shiwaliks where rivers deposit pebbles is:
questions.explanation: Bhabar is a narrow belt where rivers descending from mountains deposit pebbles.

questions.text: The wet, swampy and marshy region south of the Bhabar belt is known as:
questions.explanation: South of Bhabar, the streams re-emerge and create a wet, swampy and marshy region known as Terai.

questions.text: The largest part of the northern plain is formed of older alluvium, known as:
questions.explanation: Bhangar is the older alluvium that lies above the floodplains of the rivers.

questions.text: The newer, younger deposits of the flood plains are called:
questions.explanation: Khadar are the younger deposits, renewed almost every year and very fertile.

questions.text: The Peninsular plateau is a tableland composed of:
options: Old crystalline, igneous and metamorphic rocks
questions.explanation: The Peninsular plateau was formed due to the breaking and drifting of the Gondwana land.

questions.text: Which part of the plateau lies to the north of the Narmada river covering a major area of the Malwa plateau?
questions.explanation: The part of the Peninsular plateau lying to the north of the Narmada river is the Central Highlands.

questions.text: The eastward extensions of the Central Highlands are locally known as:
questions.explanation: The Bundelkhand and Baghelkhand are the local names for the eastward extensions of the Central Highlands.

questions.text: Which plateau marks the further eastward extension, drained by the Damodar river?
questions.explanation: The Chotanagpur plateau marks the further eastward extension of the Central Highlands.

questions.text: The Deccan Plateau is a ________ landmass that lies to the south of the river Narmada.
questions.explanation: The Deccan Plateau is a triangular landmass south of the Narmada.

questions.text: Which mountain range marks the northern base of the Deccan Plateau?
questions.explanation: The Satpura range flanks its broad base in the north.

questions.text: Western Ghats are ________ than the Eastern Ghats.
questions.explanation: Western Ghats are higher (900-1600m) compared to Eastern Ghats (600m).

questions.text: What is the highest peak in the Western Ghats?
questions.explanation: Anai Mudi (2,695 m) is the highest peak in the Western Ghats.

questions.text: Which of the following is the highest peak in the Eastern Ghats?
questions.explanation: Mahendragiri (1,501 metres) is the highest peak in the Eastern Ghats.

questions.text: The black soil area of the Peninsular plateau is known as:
questions.explanation: One of the distinct features of the Peninsular plateau is the black soil area known as Deccan Trap.

questions.text: The Indian Desert lies towards the western margins of which hills?
questions.explanation: The Indian desert lies towards the western margins of the Aravalli Hills.

questions.text: Crescent-shaped dunes found in the Indian desert are called:
questions.explanation: Barchans are crescent-shaped dunes that cover larger areas of the desert.

questions.text: Which is the only large river in the Indian Desert region?
questions.explanation: Luni is the only large river in this arid region.

questions.text: The western coastal strip, south of Goa, is referred to as:
questions.explanation: The central stretch of the western coast is called the Kannad Plain.

questions.text: The northern part of the western coast is called:
questions.explanation: The northern part of the western coast is called Konkan (Mumbai – Goa).

questions.text: The southern part of the eastern coast is known as:
questions.explanation: The southern part of the eastern coast is referred to as the Coromandel Coast.

questions.text: Which is the largest salt water lake in India, located in Odisha?
questions.explanation: The Chilika Lake is the largest salt water lake in India.

questions.text: Lakshadweep Islands are composed of small ________ islands.
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep islands are small coral islands.

questions.text: What was the earlier name of Lakshadweep?
questions.explanation: Earlier they were known as Laccadive, Minicoy and Amindive. In 1973, they were named Lakshadweep.

questions.text: Which island in the Lakshadweep group has a bird sanctuary and is uninhabited?
questions.explanation: The Pitti island, which is uninhabited, has a bird sanctuary.

questions.text: India’s only active volcano is found on which island?
questions.explanation: India’s only active volcano is found on Barren island in Andaman and Nicobar group of islands.

questions.text: Which feature of India provides a site for fishing and port activities?
questions.explanation: The coastal plains provide sites for fishing and port activities.

questions.text: The Himalayas are also known as:
questions.explanation: The Himalayas represent youthful topography with high peaks and deep valleys, formed recently in geological history.

questions.text: According to the Theory of Plate Tectonics, the earth’s crust is formed of ________ major plates.
questions.explanation: The earth’s crust has been formed out of seven major and some minor plates.
